sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund 12 months of Eternl operations, maintenance, and improvements, including frontend and backend work, infrastructure, support, administration, and audits/oversight.

Deliverables: Frontend maintenance and cross-platform development, Backend infrastructure and operations, User support, Administrative operations, Periodic independent audits of treasury fund use and oversight metrics, Eternl Pro plan rollout and related repayment reporting
Deadline/expiry: Delivery period August 2026 to July 2027

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover approximately $420,000 in annual costs for a 12-month operating period.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal funds 6.0 FTE distributed across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Eternl states it has about 100,000 browser extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposer claims that if about 4.2% of the install base, or roughly 5,500 users, buy the Pro plan, Pro income alone would fully cover the annual $420,000 cost.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposer commits to convert up to $420,000 worth of Ada into stablecoins, deposit it into a public company wallet, and return any Ada above that amount to the treasury.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposer commits to assess repayment every December and June and use surplus treasury stablecoins plus paid plan income above $420,000 to repay the treasury until $420,000 in Ada value has been repaid.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal funds 6.0 FTE distributed across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE.
Independent evidence for: Eternl states it has about 100,000 browser extension installs and about 30,000 Android and iOS installs.
Independent evidence for: Eternl claims 10-18% of Cardano mainnet transactions are conducted via Eternl.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states that without enough funding or paid license sales beyond August, it may let developers go, scale Eternl down to essential maintenance, shift focus outside Cardano, or remove the free option.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Eternl requests ₳2,350,000, valued at about $0.1787 per Ada, to cover approximately $420,000 in annual costs for a 12-month operating period." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal funds 6.0 FTE distributed across 10 contributors at $70,000 per FTE." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
